ip_outcome_note: Norin, Swift Survivalist is canonically the same character as Norin the Wary (Time Spiral, 2006). Scryfall type_line confirms 'Legendary Creature — Human Coward' — the Coward subtype is a direct callback to the original's flavor identity. The character is Wizards/MTG-internal (Dominaria-born human warrior, no external IP). No ## IP block required. The 0297 collector number is the borderless/variant printing of DSK #145.

trivia_bullets.text: Norin was first referenced in flavor text on Jade Statue from Limited Edition Alpha (1993) — one of the earliest named characters in Magic lore — before receiving his own card in Time Spiral (2006, collector no. 171). His Duskmourn card marks the first time he has been reprinted as a new legendary since then, nearly 18 years later. [Scryfall: Norin the Wary, TSP #171; WebSearch: magicspoiler.com]

The card's story conceit is that Norin — infamous on Dominaria for fleeing every combat — literally stumbled through an Omenpath door and found himself inside Duskmourn, the sentient horror-plane ruled by Valgavoth. His subtype was upgraded from Human Warrior (TSP) to Human Coward in DSK, a deliberate creature-type callback encoding his defining characteristic. [WebSearch: magicspoiler.com; Scryfall: DSK #145 type_line]

Where Norin the Wary exiles himself whenever any player casts a spell or a creature attacks, making him notoriously difficult to interact with (EDHREC rank 4956 on the original), Norin, Swift Survivalist inverts the cowardice into aggressive utility: he can attack, and when blocked he may exile the blocked creature to replay it immediately, enabling haste re-entry or dodge-and-redeploy tactics. The shift is thematic — even Norin adapts to survive in Duskmourn. [Scryfall: Norin the Wary oracle_text; Scryfall: Norin Swift Survivalist oracle_text; EDHREC rank from Scryfall edhrec_rank field]

Flavor text: "I don't know what it was! Why would I stick around to find out?" — unnamed speaker (Norin), [Scryfall]. The original Norin the Wary's flavor text reads: "I have a bad feeling about this." [Scryfall: TSP #171]. Both cards lean on the same deadpan-coward voice register.

The 0297 collector number on this copy identifies it as the borderless showcase/variant printing within DSK; the base version is DSK #145. Both printings carry the same oracle text and are legal in the same formats. [Scryfall: collector_number field; retailer listings at nobleknight.com and thegamecornergames.com confirm 0297 variant exists as a separate SKU]
